name: Cañada Real de Salinilla Trail
name_original: Castillo Medieval de Gigonza desde Paterna de Rivera
description: Hike to the historic Medieval Castle of Gigonza from Paterna de Rivera, a moderate 7.2-mile trail through scenic rural landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Paterna De Rivera offers diverse landscapes, from dense cork oak forests to open pasturelands and significant limestone formations. The region is characterized by varied topography, including mountains, valleys, and rivers, providing a range of natural features for exploration. Hikers can experience both lush vegetation and historical sites, such as ancient castles and thermal springs. This blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage makes it a compelling destination for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Paterna De Rivera The most popular hiking route is Cañada Real de Salinilla Trail, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route follows a historic path through rural landscapes, leading towards the area of Gigonza. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gigonza Castle loop from Hoyo de la Cuna, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.5 km) path. This trail explores the countryside around the historic Gigonza Castle, offering views of the ancient fortress and surrounding terrain. Local hikers also love the Gigonza Castle – Sierra Alta loop from Paterna de Rivera, a 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain including hills and pasturelands, often completed in about 3 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Paterna De Rivera is defined by extensive cork oak forests, open pasturelands, and prominent limestone rock formations. The network offers options for easy walks and moderate full-day hikes. The routes in Paterna De Rivera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Paterna De Rivera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Paterna De Rivera?
A: Hiking around Paterna De Rivera offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter dense cork oak forests, particularly within the nearby Los Alcornocales Natural Park, along with open pasturelands, valleys, and rivers. The region also features significant limestone rock formations, providing varied scenery for your walks.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, there are easy trails available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33900129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Medina Sidonia&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.9 km path that takes approximately 1 hour 22 minutes to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's scenery.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Paterna De Rivera area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14568126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gigonza Castle loop from Hoyo de la Cuna&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.5 km circular path exploring the countryside around the historic castle. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31539930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gigonza Castle – Sierra Alta loop from Paterna de Rivera&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 13.2 km trail through varied terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I discover on the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural features. You can explore the extensive cork oak forests of Los Alcornocales Natural Park, which also features humid 'canutos' (semi-tropical laurel forests), streams, and potential waterfalls. The impressive limestone formation of Peña Arpada, a natural monument, is also nearby, offering scenic views.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. The area is home to historical sites such as the Medieval Castle of Gigonza, which can be explored on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995867868/canada-real-de-salinilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Real de Salinilla Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has historic thermal springs, the Healing Baths of Gigonza and Fuente Santa, appreciated since Roman times.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Paterna De Rivera?
A: The trails around Paterna De Rivera are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from dense forests to open pasturelands, and the opportunity to experience both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Is it possible to see wildlife while hiking in this region?
A: Yes, the Los Alcornocales Natural Park, a primary draw for hikers near Paterna de Rivera, is known for its rich biodiversity. You might spot wildlife such as wildcats, foxes, deer, wild boars, and various raptors within the park's diverse habitats.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views?
A: While specific viewpoint trails are not detailed, the varied topography of Los Alcornocales Natural Park, with its mountains and valleys, suggests routes within the park offer impressive panoramic views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38257008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Medina Sidonia loop from Medina Sidonia&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic vistas of the surrounding area.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in Paterna De Rivera?
A: The hiking trails around Paterna De Rivera cater to various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix of easy walks and moderate full-day hikes. There are currently no routes classified as difficult, making the area accessible for most hikers.

Q: Can I observe fighting bulls in their natural environment while hiking?
A: Yes, Paterna de Rivera is part of the famous Ruta del Toro (Bull Route). This scenic route traverses pasturelands where you can observe fighting bulls in their natural ranching environment, combining wildlife observation with the appreciation of traditional Andalusian countryside.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Paterna De Rivera area?
A: There are currently 7 hiking routes available around Paterna De Rivera, ranging from easy to moderate difficulty, offering a variety of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts.
tips:
One of the best preserved specimens in the region and that is how they try to keep it by avoiding major restoration works.
The castle is of Andalusian origin and its architecture clearly indicates this. It has a rectangular floor plan with a 2-storey tower surrounded by a central square and the main access door that bears the Casa de Arcos coat of arms.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the hike to Gigonza Castle?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered a moderate hike. While it involves an elevation gain of approximately 180 meters, the paths are generally accessible, making it suitable for hikers with a good level of fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The path features gentle sandy tracks that ascend and descend. Much of the route follows a 'vía pecuaria' or livestock trail, maintaining its original width. You'll find it often runs between fenced estates and is characterized by scattered large stones.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a scenic rural landscape with holm oaks and cork oaks, passing through dehesa fields where you might spot various livestock like goats, sheep, and cows. The primary highlight is the ancient Gigonza Castle itself, and you'll also see views of Paterna de Rivera and Medina Sidonia in the distance.

faqs.question: Are there any historical points of interest besides the castle?
faqs.answer: Yes, near the Gigonza Castle, you can find the ruins of sulfurous water baths. These baths were historically renowned in the late 19th and early 20th centuries for their medicinal properties, adding another layer of history to your hike.

faqs.question: Is the trail to Gigonza Castle dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through rural landscapes and dehesa fields where livestock graze. While dogs are generally allowed, it's advisable to keep them on a leash, especially when near animals, to ensure their safety and respect the local environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers pleasant conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. These seasons provide comfortable hiking weather and showcase the natural beauty of the holm and cork oak landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Paterna de Rivera?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Paterna de Rivera is the starting point. Look for available parking within the town, particularly near the beginning of the route, to access the trail conveniently.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or visit the Gigonza Castle area. It's freely accessible for hikers.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and approximately 11.6 km length, the trail is best suited for families with children who are accustomed to hiking and have a good level of fitness. The varied terrain and historical points of interest can make it an engaging experience for active families.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Paterna de Rivera?
faqs.answer: Paterna de Rivera is a local town, and you can expect to find amenities such as cafes, pubs, and restaurants there. It's a good idea to check local listings for options before or after your hike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the hike?
faqs.answer: The hike to the Medieval Castle of Gigonza from Paterna de Rivera typically takes around 3 hours and 10 minutes to complete, covering a distance of approximately 11.6 kilometers.
